name: First White House of the Confederacy
description: Official residence of Jefferson Davis during Montgomery's tenure as the first Confederate capital (Feb-May 1861). Two-story Italianate home with period furnishings and Civil War artifacts.

name: Alabama State Capitol
description: Where Jefferson Davis was inaugurated as Confederate president and where the Selma to Montgomery marches concluded in 1965. National Historic Landmark with stunning Greek Revival architecture.

name: Freedom Riders Bus Station
description: Site where Freedom Riders were attacked by a mob in 1961 while testing desegregation of interstate travel. Now a museum dedicated to the Freedom Riders movement.

name: Court Square Fountain
description: Historic center of Montgomery since the city's founding. Site of the major slave trading market before the Civil War and later the bus stop where Rosa Parks boarded on December 1, 1955.

name: Legacy Museum: From Enslavement to Mass Incarceration
description: Created by the Equal Justice Initiative on the site of a former slave warehouse. Chronicles the history of slavery, racial terror, segregation, and mass incarceration in America.

name: Old Alabama Town
description: Six-block historic district with over 50 restored 19th-century buildings. Working craftsmen demonstrations, guided tours, and educational programs about antebellum Montgomery life.

name: Civil Rights Memorial Center
description: Designed by Maya Lin (Vietnam Veterans Memorial architect). Features a circular black granite table with names of 40 martyrs of the Civil Rights Movement. Operated by SPLC.

name: F. Scott Fitzgerald Home
description: Author F. Scott Fitzgerald and his wife Zelda lived in this house from 1931-1932. Now a museum featuring literary artifacts and exhibits about the Jazz Age author's connection to Montgomery.
